What do we know about Northwind's reporting preferences?

Northwind prefers monthly reporting, delivered async rather than on calls I know this. Their main contact, Dana, has said twice that long status calls are a poor use of her time — she'd rather read a written summary.

From a memory captured in the 3 May client callas of 3 May 2026
Memory
What stage is the Northwind renewal deal at right now?

The Northwind renewal is at Proposal stage, $48,000 ARR, expected to close 30 June This is live.

Fetched live from HubSpot — not stored in memoryjust now
Live from system of record
What's Northwind's appetite for a multi-year deal?
I don't have durable knowledge on this

Nothing in memory or the connected systems tells me how Northwind feels about a multi-year commitment. I won't guess.

Here's what the systems of record do show: the current deal is a 12-month term at $48k ARR (HubSpot).

This question was logged as a miss — it helps the brain learn what to capture next.
Draft a renewal check-in email to Dana and prep me for the call Thursday.

On it — that's two actions. I'll use what I know about Dana's async preference for the email.

Running 2 actions
Marketer drafted a short, async-friendly check-in email used memory
Researcher compiling a meeting brief for Thursday…
Generally, what's a good cadence for SaaS renewal check-ins?

A common pattern is to start renewal conversations 90 days out, with a light touch at 90/60/30 days General inference, not from your business.

This is general knowledge — not a fact about your company. Treat accordingly.

Researcher · 5h
Report5 min ago

Competitor analysis complete

Researcher You
I've finished the competitive analysis you requested. Here are the headline findings:

1. Pricing shift — All three major competitors moved to usage-based pricing this quarter, away from seat-based.

2. Feature gaps — None of them offer the multi-agent orchestration we do. This is a clear differentiator.

3. Positioning — They're all targeting enterprise; the mid-market is underserved.

Full report with sources saved to the shared memory under "competitor-analysis-q2". Want me to turn the pricing finding into a one-pager for the sales team?
Decision Queue 1 pending
Agents are paused waiting for your input
Needs your input

Fallback chain

If the primary model is unavailable, requests fall through in order:
1Claude Opus 4.8primary
2Claude Sonnet 4.6
3GPT-4o
Notify on fallback · Max 3 retries · Restore primary when healthy

Audit Trail
Immutable, signed record of every action — references only, never content
Append-only — cannot be deleted Ed25519 signed 90-day retention
The rule: log what was touched, never the content itself. Entries record who, what action, and a reference to the affected record — so the trail can reconstruct events without becoming a second copy of sensitive data that bypasses permissions.

admin@acme.co changed role for sam@acme.co · Member → Operator

highest-value event class · IP 203.0.113.4 · signature verified ✓
09:02

reference only — content not logged · reason: superseded · signature verified ✓
08:51

Marketer (agent) wrote to HubSpot · created contact contact_4471

tool write action · reference only · signature verified ✓
08:44

sam@acme.co queried the brain · accessed memory mem_2210

query access logged by reference · not the question text · signature verified ✓
08:40

jordan@acme.co changed config retrieval_min_relevance · 0.72 → 0.75

quality-affecting · approved · reversible · signature verified ✓

What each role can do

AdminFull access — users, billing, security, all settings and integrations
OperatorRun agents, tasks, missions, crons · approve decisions · cannot manage users
MemberPersonal workspace · chat · own tasks · respond to their own inbox items
ViewerRead-only dashboards and reports · cannot create or trigger anything

Credential Vault Admin only
Encrypted secrets your agents use during tasks
Encrypted with AES-256-GCM using a key derived from your master password. The master password is never stored — changes require confirming it.

Agent Settings Admin only
Global guardrails for how all agents behave
Max parallel agents

How many agents can work at the same time. Higher means faster throughput but higher cost and load.

Max turns per task

Agents stop and ask for your review after this many back-and-forth steps on a single task.

Retries before human review

If a task fails, the agent retries this many times before escalating it to you instead of trying again.

Inactivity timeout

If an agent goes quiet for this long, the session is marked stalled and flagged for review.

sec
Quality gate (Aegis review)

Every task is reviewed by an AI judge before it can be marked done. Rejected work is sent back with feedback.

On
Verbose logging

Write the agent's full reasoning trace to the audit log. Useful for debugging, uses more storage.

Off

Guide
How everything works and why it's useful
Getting started
AI agents & skills
Tasks & priorities
Objectives & projects
Field Ops & missions
Memory & directives
Cron & automation
Security & roles
Cost management

Getting started

AIOS is your AI workforce. You give agents goals and tasks; they execute autonomously and report back. Start at the Command Centre to see fleet status, use Chat for quick questions, and the Task Board to track work. Anything pending on you surfaces in the Attention Required panel — check there first each day.

Memory & directives

Memory is what makes agents feel like they know your business. Facts are stored and reinforced over time. Directives are hard rules injected into every agent — add "never discuss pricing publicly" and it takes effect immediately, no redeployment. Mental Models are cached answers to standing questions like "what are our common support issues?".

Security & roles

Every action is logged and signed. Agents earn a trust score that drops on failures or blocked threats; low-trust agents are auto-quarantined. Four roles control access: Admin (everything), Operator (run the system), Member (personal workspace), and Viewer (read-only).

Quality Monitor
Catching silent failure — when the brain is confidently wrong
The dominant risk is the brain being fluent and wrong. These metrics watch for quality eroding silently — the numbers that move before anyone notices a bad answer.
Silent-failure signals
11%
Abstention rate
healthy — holding steady
6.2%
Memory miss rate
rising — watch
3.1%
Low-rated answers
within range
0.78
Avg retrieval quality
above floor 0.72

Abstention rate over time

A rising abstention rate is good — the brain is being honest about gaps. A sudden drop is the warning sign: it may have started confabulating instead of abstaining.
M
T
W
T
F
S
S
Stable around 11% — no suspicious collapse.

Memory utility distribution

Are memories being retrieved, or just accumulating? A growing pile of never-retrieved memories means the brain is hoarding, not learning.
Actively used
62%
Occasionally
26%
Never retrieved
12%
12% never-retrieved — decay will review these next run.

Memory miss rate up 1.8pts this weekMore questions the brain couldn't answer — likely a knowledge gap forming around a new client

Self-Improvement
How the system gets smarter over time
Memory consolidation pipeline · the 6 Rs
Recall

Surface relevant memories for the current task

Relate

Link new facts to existing knowledge

Refine

Merge duplicates, sharpen wording

Reduce

Prune stale or low-value memories

Reweave

Reconnect orphaned knowledge

Reflect

Synthesise higher-level insights

Suggested improvements

4 pending
Refine Marketer's prompt

3 recent rejections were tone mismatches. Suggest adding a brand-voice directive.

Consolidate 12 duplicate memories

Several facts about refund policy overlap. Merge into one canonical memory.

Downgrade summarisation model

Haiku performs identically to Sonnet on this task — saves cost with no quality loss.

Learning from outcomes

Applied directive "always cite sources" after 4 unsourced-claim rejections — 0 since

Researcher learned preferred report format from your edits — revisions down 40%

6 days ago
System Settings Admin only
Every tunable lives here — editable on the fly, but safely
The system's thresholds are configuration, not code
Gated quality-affecting changes need approvalScoped org default, override per clientBounded range-validated, can't break it audited & reversible
Memory & retrieval
Consolidation & decay
Workspace
Billing
Auth

Retrieval

retrieval_min_relevance

Cosine similarity floor. Below this, a memory is never injected — the brain abstains instead of guessing.

GatedScopedBounded
0.5–0.95
retrieval_max_results

Hard cap on memories injected per query, even if more clear the floor.

ScopedBounded
5–50

Decay & consolidation

decay_min_utility_score

Memories scoring below this are invalidated. Procedural memories (SOPs) are exempt — they leave only by replacement.

GatedBounded
0.0–0.5
decay_min_age_days

Only decay memories older than this.

Bounded
days
consolidation_dedup_similarity_threshold

Above this, a new semantic memory is treated as a near-duplicate and sent to review.

GatedBounded
0.85–0.99
consolidation_auto_merge_threshold

Above this, near-duplicates auto-merge without human review.

GatedBounded
0.95–0.999
chunk_ttl_days

Indexed-in-place chunks (RAG, not promoted to memory) are pruned after this.

ScopedBounded
days
The Self-Improvement engine can propose changes to any gated value here. You approve, it applies, the audit log records the before/after — and you can roll back anytime.

Workflow Registry
Workflow definitions across all clients — these are data, not code
200+ workflow definitions live as rows in client databases. You maintain none of them as code. A core update ships to all clients at once.
Lead qualificationtrigger: new CRM contact → researcher → scorer → email writer
NorthwindHalo+3
Meeting prep brieftrigger: calendar event 1h before → researcher → brief

Weekly client reporttrigger: Friday 5pm → analyst → report → email

Plugin Registry
Genuinely custom code — kept rare on purpose
4 plugins across 6 clients — healthy. Plugin count staying flat as clients grow is the signal the architecture is working.
meridian/custom-scorerBespoke lead-scoring formula tied to Meridian's methodology
Meridian only
northwind/legacy-intakeTransform for Northwind's legacy project system

Connections
Where the brain ingests from — and how each source is treated
Company-wide · one shared connection for the whole business · ingests broadly, visibility still per-permission
HubSpot CRMdeal stages, contacts, pipeline — fetched live
Structured · live Connected
Slack workspacechannels ingested for interpretive knowledge (excl. #personal)
Unstructured · interpretive Connected
Asanatasks, projects — fetched live
Structured · live Connected
Per-user · each person connects their own · one credential row per user · only they see their data
Gmail42 of 42 users connected · interpretive only
Unstructured · interpretive Org rollout
Google Drive38 of 42 connected · documents indexed in place
Unstructured · interpretive4 pending
Do-not-ingest · sources the brain is forbidden to touch (Gate 1)
HR mailbox · #personal · /Legal folderExcluded at the gate — never indexed, never stored
